message.content: I have several libraries that are outside of this project directory that I want to bring into this project for easier management. They are: /Users/sam/Projects/Active/swift-ovn, /Users/sam/Projects/Active/swift-qemu, /Users/sam/Projects/Active/swift-scim, /Users/sam/Projects/Active/swift-spiffe, and /Users/sam/Projects/Active/swift-ssf. Whats the best way to do this? Some are needed for the control plane and some are needed for the agent

content.text: Let me explore the current project structure and each of those external libraries to understand the situation.

1→# Strato
     2→
     3→[![Build Status](https://github.com/samcat116/strato/actions/workflows/build.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/samcat116/strato/actions/workflows/build.yaml)
     4→[![License: FSL-1.1-MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-FSL--1.1--MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE.md)
     5→[![Swift 6.0](https://img.shields.io/badge/Swift-6.0-orange.svg)](https://swift.org)
     6→[![Platform](https://img.shields.io/badge/Platform-macOS%2013%2B-lightgrey.svg)](https://www.apple.com/macos/)
     7→
     8→Strato is a fast, secure, and easy to deploy private cloud platform based on battle tested technologies and built for modern infrastructure. It enables operators to run efficient, secure, and powerful infrastructure easily.
     9→
    10→## Features
    11→
    12→- 🚀 **High Performance**: Built with Swift and Vapor for exceptional performance
    13→- 🔒 **WebAuthn/Passkey Authentication**: Modern passwordless authentication
    14→- 🏗️ **VM Management**: Full lifecycle management through Cloud Hypervisor integration
    15→- 🌐 **Modern Web Interface**: Dynamic HTMX-powered frontend with TailwindCSS
    16→- 🔐 **Fine-grained Authorization**: Powered by Permify for role-based access control
    17→- 🐳 **Container Ready**: Docker and Docker Compose support for easy deployment
    18→- 📊 **PostgreSQL Backend**: Reliable data persistence with Fluent ORM
    19→
    20→## Quick Start
    21→
    22→### Prerequisites
    23→
    24→- Swift 6.0 or later
    25→- Docker and Docker Compose
    26→- PostgreSQL (if running locally)
    27→
    28→### Using Docker Compose (Recommended)
    29→
    30→1. Clone the repository:
    31→   ```bash
    32→   git clone https://github.com/samcat116/strato.git
    33→   cd strato
    34→   ```
    35→
    36→2. Start all services:
    37→   ```bash
    38→   docker compose up app
    39→   ```
    40→
    41→3. Run database migrations:
    42→   ```bash
    43→   docker compose run migrate
    44→   ```
    45→
    46→4. Access the application at `http://localhost:8080`
    47→
    48→### Local Development
    49→
    50→1. Install dependencies:
    51→   ```bash
    52→   swift package resolve
    53→   ```
    54→
    55→2. Start supporting services:
    56→   ```bash
    57→   docker compose up db permify
    58→   ```
    59→
    60→3. Run migrations:
    61→   ```bash
    62→   swift run App migrate
    63→   ```
    64→
    65→4. Start the development server:
    66→   ```bash
    67→   swift run
    68→   ```
    69→
    70→## Core Technologies
    71→
    72→- **[Swift](https://swift.org)** - Modern, safe, and performant programming language
    73→- **[Vapor](https://vapor.codes)** - Server-side Swift web framework
    74→- **[Cloud Hypervisor](https://www.cloudhypervisor.org/)** - Modern VMM for VM management
    75→- **[PostgreSQL](https://www.postgresql.org)** - Advanced open source database
    76→- **[Permify](https://permify.co)** - Authorization service for fine-grained access control
    77→- **[HTMX](https://htmx.org)** - Dynamic web interfaces without complex JavaScript
    78→- **[TailwindCSS](https://tailwindcss.com)** - Utility-first CSS framework
    79→
    80→## Authentication
    81→
    82→Strato uses WebAuthn/Passkeys for secure, passwordless authentication. Users can register and authenticate using:
    83→
    84→- Security keys (YubiKey, etc.)
    85→- Platform authenticators (Touch ID, Face ID, Windows Hello)
    86→- Cross-platform authenticators
    87→
    88→## Project Structure
    89→
    90→```
    91→strato/
    92→├── Sources/App/           # Swift application source
    93→│   ├── Controllers/       # HTTP request handlers
    94→│   ├── Models/           # Database models
    95→│   ├── Services/         # Business logic services
    96→│   └── configure.swift   # Application configuration
    97→├── Resources/Views/      # Leaf templates
    98→├── Public/              # Static assets
    99→├── web/                 # HTMX templates and components
   100→├── permify/             # Authorization schema and config
   101→└── docker-compose.yml   # Development environment
   102→```
   103→
   104→## Architecture
   105→
   106→For detailed technical architecture information, see [ARCHITECTURE.md](ARCHITECTURE.md).
   107→
   108→## Development
   109→
   110→### Available Commands
   111→
   112→- `swift build` - Build the application
   113→- `swift test` - Run tests
   114→- `swift run` - Start the application
   115→- `docker compose build` - Build Docker images
   116→- `docker compose up app` - Start with all dependencies
   117→- `docker compose run migrate` - Run database migrations
   118→
   119→### Environment Variables
   120→
   121→Key configuration options:
   122→
   123→- `DATABASE_URL` - PostgreSQL connection string
   124→- `PERMIFY_ENDPOINT` - Permify service endpoint
   125→- `WEBAUTHN_RELYING_PARTY_ID` - WebAuthn relying party identifier
   126→- `WEBAUTHN_RELYING_PARTY_ORIGIN` - WebAuthn origin URL
   127→
   128→## Contributing
   129→
   130→1. Fork the repository
   131→2. Create a feature branch: `git checkout -b feature/amazing-feature`
   132→3. Make your changes and add tests
   133→4. Ensure all tests pass: `swift test`
   134→5. Submit a pull request
   135→
   136→## License
   137→

   139→
   140→## Support

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive report.

## Strato Project Structure - Complete Exploration Report

### 1. Top-Level Directory Structure

The Strato project is located at `/Users/sam/Projects/Active/strato` and contains the following key directories:

**Swift Packages (Core):**
- `/agent` - CLI hypervisor agent (executable)
- `/control-plane` - Vapor web application
- `/shared` - Shared models and WebSocket DTOs

**Supporting Directories:**
- `/docs` - VitePress documentation
- `/e2e-test` - End-to-end tests
- `/helm` - Kubernetes Helm charts
- `/observability` - Observability/monitoring configuration
- `/scripts` - Build and utility scripts
- `/spicedb` - SpiceDB authorization schema
- `/tests/integration` - Integration tests
- `/vm-images` - VM image builders (alpine-3.18, ubuntu-22.04)
- `/SakeApp` - Legacy build tooling (being migrated to Taskfile)
- `/artifacts` - Build artifacts

### 2. Package.swift Files - Complete Content Analysis

#### A. Control-Plane Package (`/control-plane/Package.swift`)
- **Platform:** macOS 14+
- **Swift Tools Version:** 6.1
- **Target:** `App` (executableTarget)
- **Key Dependencies:**
  - **StratoShared** (local path: `../shared`)
  - **Vapor Framework** (4.113.0+) - Web framework
  - **Fluent** (4.9.0+) - ORM
  - **Fluent Drivers:** PostgreSQL, SQLite
  - **Elementary** (0.5.0+) - Type-safe HTML DSL
  - **Elementary-HTMX** (0.4.0+) - HTMX integration
  - **swift-nio** (2.71.0+) - Networking
  - **WebAuthn** (main branch) - Passkey authentication
  - **swift-scim** (main branch, samcat116 fork) - SCIM protocol support
  - **JWT** (4.0.0+) - Token handling
  - **AsyncHTTPClient** (1.9.0+) - HTTP operations
  - **swift-crypto** (3.0.0+) - Cryptographic operations
  - **OpenAPI Generator & Runtime** (1.2.0+, 1.0.0+) - Spec-first API
  - **swift-openapi-vapor** (1.0.0+) - Vapor OpenAPI bindings
  - **swift-otel** (1.0.0+) - OpenTelemetry observability

#### B. Agent Package (`/agent/Package.swift`)
- **Platform:** macOS 14+
- **Swift Tools Version:** 6.0
- **Targets:** `StratoAgent` (executable) and `StratoAgentCore` (library)
- **Key Dependencies:**
  - **StratoShared** (local path: `../shared`)
  - **swift-qemu** (main branch, samcat116 fork) - QEMU hypervisor integration
  - **swift-nio** (2.65.0+) - Networking
  - **WebSocketKit** (2.0.0+) - WebSocket client
  - **ArgumentParser** (1.2.0+) - CLI argument handling
  - **swift-log** (1.5.0+) - Logging
  - **swift-ovn** (main branch, samcat116 fork) - OVN/OVS networking (Linux only)
  - **swift-toml** (master branch, samcat116 fork) - TOML config parsing
  - **swift-testing** (0.10.0+) - Testing framework

**Platform-Specific Dependencies:**
- **Linux:** SwiftQEMU + SwiftOVN (for OVN/OVS networking)
- **macOS:** SwiftQEMU only (user-mode networking, no OVN/OVS)

#### C. Shared Package (`/shared/Package.swift`)
- **Platform:** macOS 14+, iOS 13+
- **Swift Tools Version:** 6.0
- **Target:** `StratoShared` (library)
- **Minimal Dependencies:**
  - **swift-nio** (2.65.0+) - Only for NIOCore and NIOWebSocket (WebSocket support)
- **Products:** Exports `StratoShared` library for use by control-plane and agent

### 3. Dependency Summary - External Swift Packages

**Direct Dependencies Declared:**

| Package | Source | Version | Used By | Purpose |
|---------|--------|---------|---------|---------|
| swift-scim | samcat116/swift-scim (main) | main branch | control-plane | SCIM protocol implementation |
| swift-qemu | samcat116/swift-qemu (main) | main branch | agent | QEMU hypervisor bindings |
| swift-ovn | samcat116/swift-ovn (main) | main branch | agent (Linux only) | OVN/OVS networking |
| swift-toml | samcat116/swift-toml (master) | master branch | agent | TOML config file parsing |
| swift-nio | apple/swift-nio | 2.65.0-2.71.0+ | all packages | Networking primitives |
| Vapor | vapor/vapor | 4.113.0+ | control-plane | Web framework |
| Fluent | vapor/fluent | 4.9.0+ | control-plane | ORM |
| fluent-postgres-driver | vapor/fluent-postgres-driver | 2.8.0+ | control-plane | PostgreSQL ORM driver |
| fluent-sqlite-driver | vapor/fluent-sqlite-driver | 4.6.0+ | control-plane | SQLite ORM driver |
| Elementary | sliemeobn/elementary | 0.5.0+ | control-plane | Type-safe HTML DSL |
| elementary-htmx | sliemeobn/elementary-htmx | 0.4.0+ | control-plane | HTMX integration |
| WebAuthn | swift-server/webauthn-swift | main | control-plane | WebAuthn/Passkey auth |
| JWT | vapor/jwt | 4.0.0+ | control-plane | JWT tokens |
| AsyncHTTPClient | swift-server/async-http-client | 1.9.0+ | control-plane | HTTP client |
| swift-crypto | apple/swift-crypto | 3.0.0+ | control-plane | Cryptography |
| swift-openapi-generator | apple/swift-openapi-generator | 1.2.0+ | control-plane | OpenAPI code gen |
| swift-openapi-runtime | apple/swift-openapi-runtime | 1.0.0+ | control-plane | OpenAPI runtime |
| swift-openapi-vapor | swift-server/swift-openapi-vapor | 1.0.0+ | control-plane | Vapor OpenAPI bindings |
| swift-otel | swift-otel/swift-otel | 1.0.0+ | control-plane | OpenTelemetry observability |
| WebSocketKit | vapor/websocket-kit | 2.0.0+ | agent | WebSocket client |
| ArgumentParser | apple/swift-argument-parser | 1.2.0+ | agent | CLI arguments |
| swift-log | apple/swift-log | 1.5.0+ | agent | Structured logging |
| swift-testing | apple/swift-testing | 0.10.0+ | agent | Testing framework |

**Search Results:** No references to `swift-spiffe` or `swift-ssf` found in the codebase.

### 4. Monorepo Structure Overview

```
strato/
├── agent/
│   ├── Package.swift
│   ├── Sources/
│   │   ├── StratoAgent/          # Main agent executable
│   │   │   ├── StratoAgent.swift
│   │   │   ├── Agent.swift
│   │   │   ├── WebSocketClient.swift
│   │   │   ├── QEMUService.swift
│   │   │   ├── NetworkServiceProtocol.swift
│   │   │   ├── NetworkServiceLinux.swift    # Platform-specific
│   │   │   └── NetworkServiceMacOS.swift    # Platform-specific
│   │   └── StratoAgentCore/     # Testable core library
│   │       ├── AgentConfig.swift
│   │       └── CustomLogHandler.swift
│   └── Tests/
│
├── control-plane/
│   ├── Package.swift
│   ├── Sources/App/             # Vapor application
│   │   ├── entrypoint.swift
│   │   ├── Middleware/          # Auth, authorization
│   │   ├── Migrations/          # Database schema
│   │   ├── Models/              # Data models
│   │   ├── Controllers/         # HTTP handlers
│   │   └── Services/            # Business logic
│   ├── Resources/
│   │   └── Views/               # Leaf templates
│   ├── web/                     # HTMX templates
│   ├── Public/                  # Static assets
│   └── Tests/
│
├── shared/
│   ├── Package.swift
│   └── Sources/StratoShared/    # Shared models & DTOs
│       ├── NetworkModels.swift
│       ├── WebSocketProtocol.swift
│       ├── VMModels.swift
│       └── QEMUDTOs.swift
│
├── docs/                        # VitePress documentation
├── e2e-test/                    # End-to-end tests
├── helm/                        # Kubernetes Helm charts
├── observability/               # Monitoring/observability config
├── scripts/                     # Build scripts
├── spicedb/                     # Authorization schema
├── tests/integration/           # Integration tests
├── vm-images/                   # VM builders
└── [config files]
    ├── Taskfile.yml             # Task orchestration (primary)
    ├── package.json             # NPM scripts
    ├── docker-compose.yml       # Local dev environment
    └── SakeApp/                 # Legacy task runner (deprecated)
```

### 5. Key Technologies & Patterns

**Architecture:**
- **Monorepo with three interdependent Swift packages**
- **Control-plane:** Server-side Vapor web application with PostgreSQL
- **Agent:** Platform-specific hypervisor agent (macOS HVF, Linux KVM/OVN)
- **Shared:** Common models, WebSocket protocol definitions, DTOs

**Platform Support:**
- Primary: macOS 14+ (development, HVF hypervisor)
- Secondary: Linux (OVN networking, KVM hypervisor)

**Authentication & Authorization:**
- WebAuthn/Passkeys (modern passwordless)
- SCIM protocol (identity federation)
- SpiceDB (fine-grained authorization)

**VM/Network Management:**
- QEMU hypervisor integration via swift-qemu
- OVN/OVS networking (Linux) via swift-ovn
- User-mode networking (macOS)

**Build & Development:**
- Task runner: `Taskfile.yml` (primary orchestration)
- Legacy: `SakeApp/` (being migrated)
- Commands: `task dev`, `task start-control-plane`, `task start-agent`, etc.

### 6. Git Repository Information

- **Remote:** git@github.com:samcat116/strato.git
- **Current Branch:** feat/macOS-mvp
- **Main Branch:** main
- **Recent Activity:** Taskfile migration, dev auth bypass, Next.js frontend

### Summary

The Strato project is a sophisticated private cloud platform with three main Swift packages following a monorepo pattern. It uses **custom forks** of swift-qemu, swift-ovn, and swift-toml from the samcat116 GitHub user, plus swift-scim for SCIM protocol support. The architecture cleanly separates the server (control-plane), agent (hypervisor), and shared models, with platform-specific implementations for macOS and Linux. No references to swift-spiffe or swift-ssf were found in the codebase.

### 5. Key Technologies & Patterns

**Architecture:**
- **Monorepo with three interdependent Swift packages**
- **Control-plane:** Server-side Vapor web application with PostgreSQL
- **Agent:** Platform-specific hypervisor agent (macOS HVF, Linux KVM/OVN)
- **Shared:** Common models, WebSocket protocol definitions, DTOs

**Platform Support:**
- Primary: macOS 14+ (development, HVF hypervisor)
- Secondary: Linux (OVN networking, KVM hypervisor)

**Authentication & Authorization:**
- WebAuthn/Passkeys (modern passwordless)
- SCIM protocol (identity federation)
- SpiceDB (fine-grained authorization)

**VM/Network Management:**
- QEMU hypervisor integration via swift-qemu
- OVN/OVS networking (Linux) via swift-ovn
- User-mode networking (macOS)

**Build & Development:**
- Task runner: `Taskfile.yml` (primary orchestration)
- Legacy: `SakeApp/` (being migrated)
- Commands: `task dev`, `task start-control-plane`, `task start-agent`, etc.

### 6. Git Repository Information

- **Remote:** git@github.com:samcat116/strato.git
- **Current Branch:** feat/macOS-mvp
- **Main Branch:** main
- **Recent Activity:** Taskfile migration, dev auth bypass, Next.js frontend

### Summary

The Strato project is a sophisticated private cloud platform with three main Swift packages following a monorepo pattern. It uses **custom forks** of swift-qemu, swift-ovn, and swift-toml from the samcat116 GitHub user, plus swift-scim for SCIM protocol support. The architecture cleanly separates the server (control-plane), agent (hypervisor), and shared models, with platform-specific implementations for macOS and Linux. No references to swift-spiffe or swift-ssf were found in the codebase.

The monorepo at /Users/sam/Projects/Active/strato already has 3 Swift packages:
- `control-plane/` - Vapor web app (Package.swift with swift-tools-version 6.1)
- `agent/` - CLI hypervisor agent (Package.swift with swift-tools-version 6.0)
- `shared/` - Shared models library (Package.swift with swift-tools-version 6.0)

The `shared` package is already consumed as a local path dependency (`../shared`) by both control-plane and agent.

## Libraries to Bring In
1. **swift-ovn** (SwiftOVN) - Currently at /Users/sam/Projects/Active/swift-ovn. Used by **agent** (Linux only). Depends on swift-nio, swift-log. Swift tools 5.9.
2. **swift-qemu** (SwiftQEMU) - Currently at /Users/sam/Projects/Active/swift-qemu. Used by **agent**. Depends on swift-nio, swift-log. Swift tools 6.0.
3. **swift-scim** (SwiftSCIM) - Currently at /Users/sam/Projects/Active/swift-scim. Used by **control-plane**. Zero external deps. Swift tools 6.1.
4. **swift-spiffe** - Currently at /Users/sam/Projects/Active/swift-spiffe. Incomplete - only has .claude/ dir, no Package.swift or source code. Not currently referenced in any Package.swift.
5. **swift-ssf** (SwiftSSF) - Currently at /Users/sam/Projects/Active/swift-ssf. NOT currently referenced in strato. Depends on swift-nio, swift-crypto, swift-log, async-http-client, swift-nio-http2. Swift tools 5.9.

## Current Dependency Declarations
In agent/Package.swift:
- swift-qemu: `.package(url: "https://github.com/samcat116/swift-qemu.git", branch: "main")`
- swift-ovn: `.package(url: "https://github.com/samcat116/swift-ovn.git", branch: "main")` (Linux only)

In control-plane/Package.swift:
- swift-scim: `.package(url: "https://github.com/samcat116/swift-scim.git", branch: "main")`

## Key Observations
- None of these libraries depend on each other
- They all use common Apple/Swift-server ecosystem deps (swift-nio, swift-log, etc.)
- The monorepo already has the pattern of local path dependencies (shared)
- swift-spiffe is basically empty, swift-ssf is not yet used in the project

## Question
What is the best approach to bring these libraries into the monorepo? Consider:
1. Directory structure (where to place them)
2. How to update Package.swift files to use local paths instead of git URLs
3. Whether to keep them as separate SPM packages or merge them
4. Git history preservation options
5. Any concerns about swift-tools-version mismatches (5.9 vs 6.0 vs 6.1)
6. What to do about swift-spiffe (empty) and swift-ssf (not yet used)

Design the cleanest approach that follows the existing monorepo patterns.

     9→    ],
    10→    products: [

    12→            name: "StratoShared",
    13→            targets: ["StratoShared"]
    14→        ),
    15→    ],
    16→    dependencies: [

    19→    ],
    20→    targets: [

    22→            name: "StratoShared",

    26→            ]
    27→        )
    28→    ],
    29→    swiftLanguageModes: [.v6]
    30→)

1→// swift-tools-version:6.0
     2→import PackageDescription
     3→
     4→let package = Package(
     5→    name: "strato-agent",
     6→    platforms: [
     7→        .macOS(.v14)
     8→    ],
     9→    dependencies: [
    10→        // StratoShared for common models and protocols
    11→        .package(path: "../shared"),
    12→        .package(url: "https://github.com/samcat116/swift-qemu", branch: "main"),
    13→        .package(url: "https://github.com/apple/swift-nio.git", from: "2.65.0"),
    14→        .package(url: "https://github.com/vapor/websocket-kit.git", from: "2.0.0"),
    15→        .package(url: "https://github.com/apple/swift-argument-parser.git", from: "1.2.0"),
    16→        .package(url: "https://github.com/apple/swift-log.git", from: "1.5.0"),
    17→        .package(url: "https://github.com/samcat116/swift-ovn.git", branch: "main"),
    18→        .package(url: "https://github.com/samcat116/swift-toml.git", branch: "master"),
    19→        .package(url: "https://github.com/apple/swift-testing.git", from: "0.10.0"),
    20→    ],
    21→    targets: [
    22→        // Core library with testable code (no SwiftQEMU dependency)
    23→        .target(
    24→            name: "StratoAgentCore",
    25→            dependencies: [
    26→                .product(name: "Logging", package: "swift-log"),
    27→                .product(name: "Toml", package: "swift-toml"),
    28→            ],
    29→            path: "Sources/StratoAgentCore",
    30→            swiftSettings: swiftSettings
    31→        ),
    32→        .executableTarget(
    33→            name: "StratoAgent",
    34→            dependencies: [
    35→                "StratoAgentCore",
    36→                .product(name: "StratoShared", package: "shared"),
    37→                .product(name: "NIOCore", package: "swift-nio"),
    38→                .product(name: "NIOPosix", package: "swift-nio"),
    39→                .product(name: "WebSocketKit", package: "websocket-kit"),
    40→                .product(name: "ArgumentParser", package: "swift-argument-parser"),
    41→                .product(name: "Logging", package: "swift-log"),
    42→            ] + qemuAndNetworkDependencies,
    43→            swiftSettings: swiftSettings
    44→        ),
    45→        .testTarget(
    46→            name: "StratoAgentTests",
    47→            dependencies: [
    48→                "StratoAgentCore",
    49→                .product(name: "Testing", package: "swift-testing"),
    50→            ],
    51→            swiftSettings: swiftSettings
    52→        )
    53→    ],
    54→    swiftLanguageModes: [.v5]
    55→)
    56→
    57→var swiftSettings: [SwiftSetting] {
    58→    []
    59→}
    60→
    61→// Conditional dependencies based on platform
    62→// SwiftQEMU: Available on both Linux (KVM) and macOS (HVF)
    63→// SwiftOVN: Linux only (OVN/OVS not available on macOS)
    64→#if os(Linux)
    65→var qemuAndNetworkDependencies: [Target.Dependency] {
    66→    [
    67→        .product(name: "SwiftQEMU", package: "swift-qemu"),
    68→        .product(name: "SwiftOVN", package: "swift-ovn"),
    69→    ]
    70→}
    71→#else
    72→var qemuAndNetworkDependencies: [Target.Dependency] {
    73→    // macOS: SwiftQEMU with HVF support, user-mode networking (no OVN/OVS)
    74→    [
    75→        .product(name: "SwiftQEMU", package: "swift-qemu"),
    76→    ]
    77→}
    78→#endif
    79→

    56→                .product(name: "SwiftSCIM", package: "swift-scim"),

1→version: "3"
     2→
     3→vars:
     4→  PROJECT_ROOT:
     5→    sh: pwd
     6→  POSTGRES_CONTAINER: strato-postgres
     7→  SPICEDB_CONTAINER: strato-spicedb
     8→  POSTGRES_DB: vapor_database
     9→  POSTGRES_USER: vapor_username
    10→  POSTGRES_PASSWORD: vapor_password
    11→  SPICEDB_KEY: strato-dev-key
    12→
    13→env:
    14→  DATABASE_HOST: localhost
    15→  DATABASE_PORT: "5432"
    16→  DATABASE_NAME: vapor_database
    17→  DATABASE_USERNAME: vapor_username
    18→  DATABASE_PASSWORD: vapor_password
    19→  SPICEDB_ENDPOINT: http://localhost:8081
    20→  SPICEDB_PRESHARED_KEY: strato-dev-key
    21→  WEBAUTHN_RELYING_PARTY_ID: localhost
    22→  WEBAUTHN_RELYING_PARTY_NAME: Strato
    23→  WEBAUTHN_RELYING_PARTY_ORIGIN: http://localhost:8080
    24→  OTEL_LOGS_ENABLED: "false"
    25→  STRATO_IMAGE_DIR: "{{.PROJECT_ROOT}}/vm-images"
    26→
    27→tasks:
    28→  # ============================================================================
    29→  # Main Development Task
    30→  # ============================================================================
    31→  dev:
    32→    desc: Start complete development environment
    33→    cmds:
    34→      - task: start-postgres
    35→      - task: start-spicedb
    36→      - task: load-spicedb-schema
    37→      - task: start-control-plane
    38→      - task: create-agent-config
    39→      - task: create-agent-token
    40→      - task: start-agent
    41→      - task: create-test-vm

     3→# ================================

     5→
     6→# Install OS updates
     7→RUN export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive DEBCONF_NONINTERACTIVE_SEEN=true \
     8→    && apt-get -q update \
     9→    && apt-get -q dist-upgrade -y \
    10→    && apt-get install -y libjemalloc-dev
    11→
    12→# Set up a build area
    13→WORKDIR /build
    14→
    15→# Copy shared package first
    16→COPY ./shared ./shared
    17→
    18→# Copy agent package files
    19→COPY ./agent/Package.* ./agent/
    20→
    21→# First just resolve dependencies.
    22→# This creates a cached layer that can be reused
    23→# as long as your Package.swift/Package.resolved
    24→# files do not change.
    25→WORKDIR /build/agent
    26→# Update Package.swift to use absolute path for shared package in Docker
    27→RUN sed -i 's|.package(path: "../shared")|.package(path: "/build/shared")|' Package.swift
    28→RUN swift package resolve \
    29→    $([ -f ./Package.resolved ] && echo "--force-resolved-versions" || true)
    30→
    31→# Copy entire agent repo into container
    32→COPY ./agent .
    33→
    34→# Build the application statically, with optimizations and using jemalloc
    35→RUN swift build -c release \
    36→    --product StratoAgent \
    37→    -Xlinker -ljemalloc
    38→
    39→# Switch to the staging area
    40→WORKDIR /staging
    41→
    42→# Copy main executable to staging area
    43→RUN cp "$(swift build --package-path /build/agent -c release --show-bin-path)/StratoAgent" ./
    44→
    45→# Copy static swift backtracer binary to staging area (try both possible locations)
    46→RUN cp "/usr/lib/swift/linux/swift-backtrace-static" ./ 2>/dev/null || \
    47→    cp "/usr/libexec/swift/linux/swift-backtrace-static" ./ 2>/dev/null || \
    48→    echo "Warning: swift-backtrace-static not found"
    49→
    50→# Copy resources bundled by SPM to staging area
    51→RUN find -L "$(swift build --package-path /build/agent -c release --show-bin-path)/" -regex '.*\.resources$' -exec cp -Ra {} ./ \;
    52→
    53→# ================================
    54→# Run image
    55→# ================================
    56→FROM ubuntu:noble
    57→
    58→# Make sure all system packages are up to date, and install QEMU and essential packages.
    59→RUN export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive DEBCONF_NONINTERACTIVE_SEEN=true \
    60→    && apt-get -q update \
    61→    && apt-get -q dist-upgrade -y \
    62→    && apt-get -q install -y \
    63→    libjemalloc2 \
    64→    ca-certificates \
    65→    tzdata \
    66→    socat \
    67→    # QEMU and KVM for virtualization
    68→    qemu-system-x86 \
    69→    qemu-utils \
    70→    # glib-2.0 development files for QEMUKit
    71→    libglib2.0-0 \
    72→    libglib2.0-dev \
    73→    # OVN/OVS for software-defined networking
    74→    ovn-central \
    75→    ovn-host \
    76→    ovn-common \
    77→    openvswitch-switch \
    78→    openvswitch-common \
    79→    # If your app or its dependencies import FoundationNetworking, also install `libcurl4`.
    80→    # libcurl4 \
    81→    # If your app or its dependencies import FoundationXML, also install `libxml2`.
    82→    # libxml2 \
    83→    && rm -r /var/lib/apt/lists/*
    84→
    85→# No need to copy Swift runtime libraries with static compilation
    86→
    87→# Create a strato user and group with /app as its home directory
    88→RUN useradd --user-group --create-home --system --skel /dev/null --home-dir /app strato
    89→
    90→# Switch to the new home directory
    91→WORKDIR /app
    92→
    93→# Copy built executable and any staged resources from builder
    94→COPY --from=build --chown=strato:strato /staging /app
    95→
    96→# Provide configuration needed by the built-in crash reporter and some sensible default behaviors.
    97→ENV SWIFT_BACKTRACE=enable=yes,sanitize=yes,threads=all,images=all,interactive=no,swift-backtrace=./swift-backtrace-static
    98→
    99→# Ensure all further commands run as the strato user
   100→USER strato:strato
   101→
   102→# Start the Strato Agent when the image is run
   103→ENTRYPOINT ["./StratoAgent"]
   104→CMD ["--control-plane-url", "ws://control-plane:8080/agent/ws", "--qemu-socket-dir", "/var/run/qemu"]

     2→# Build image - only for local builds
     3→# ================================
     4→FROM swift:6.1.2-noble AS build
     5→
     6→# Check if we're using prebuilt artifacts
     7→ARG PREBUILT_ARTIFACTS=""

     9→# Install OS updates and Bun for frontend build
    10→RUN export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive DEBCONF_NONINTERACTIVE_SEEN=true \
    11→    && apt-get -q update \
    12→    && apt-get -q dist-upgrade -y \
    13→    && apt-get install -y libjemalloc-dev curl unzip \
    14→    && curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash
    15→
    16→# Add Bun to PATH
    17→ENV BUN_INSTALL="/root/.bun"
    18→ENV PATH="$BUN_INSTALL/bin:$PATH"
    19→
    20→# Set up a build area
    21→WORKDIR /build
    22→
    23→# Copy shared package first
    24→COPY ./shared ./shared
    25→
    26→# Copy control plane package files
    27→COPY ./control-plane/Package.* ./control-plane/
    28→
    29→# First just resolve dependencies.
    30→WORKDIR /build/control-plane
    31→# Update Package.swift to use absolute path for shared package in Docker
    32→RUN sed -i 's|.package(path: "../shared")|.package(path: "/build/shared")|' Package.swift
    33→RUN swift package resolve \
    34→    $([ -f ./Package.resolved ] && echo "--force-resolved-versions" || true)
    35→
    36→# Copy web frontend package files
    37→COPY ./control-plane/web/package.json ./control-plane/web/bun.lock* ./web/
    38→
    39→# Install frontend dependencies
    40→WORKDIR /build/control-plane/web
    41→RUN bun install --frozen-lockfile
    42→
    43→# Copy entire control-plane repo into container
    44→WORKDIR /build/control-plane
    45→COPY ./control-plane .
    46→
    47→# Copy artifacts directory (always exists due to workflow preparation)
    48→COPY ./artifacts /build/artifacts
    49→
    50→# Build and deploy frontend (Next.js static export to Public/)
    51→WORKDIR /build/control-plane/web
    52→RUN bun run build:deploy
    53→
    54→WORKDIR /build/control-plane
    55→
    56→# Build the application only if not using prebuilt artifacts
    57→RUN if [ -z "$PREBUILT_ARTIFACTS" ]; then \
    58→        swift build -c release --product App -Xlinker -ljemalloc; \
    59→    fi

content.text:
Now I have a thorough understanding of the entire codebase. Let me provide the complete implementation plan.

---

## Implementation Plan: Bringing 5 External Swift Libraries into the Strato Monorepo

### Executive Summary

The recommended approach is to place each library as a **separate SPM package** in a new `libs/` directory at the monorepo root, mirroring the existing pattern where `shared/` is its own package consumed via `.package(path: ...)`. Each library keeps its own `Package.swift`, `Sources/`, and `Tests/`, preserving independent buildability and testability. Git history is imported using `git subtree add`.

---

### 1. Directory Structure

Proposed layout:

```
strato/
  agent/                  (existing)
  control-plane/          (existing)
  shared/                 (existing)
  libs/
    swift-ovn/
      Package.swift
      Sources/SwiftOVN/
      Tests/SwiftOVNTests/
    swift-qemu/
      Package.swift
      Sources/SwiftQEMU/
      Tests/SwiftQEMUTests/
    swift-scim/
      Package.swift
      Sources/SwiftSCIM/
      Tests/SwiftSCIMTests/
    swift-ssf/
      Package.swift
      Sources/SwiftSSF/
      Tests/SwiftSSFTests/
    swift-spiffe/         (placeholder - minimal or empty)
```

**Why `libs/` rather than flat at the root:**

- The root already has 4 top-level directories that are conceptually "the app" (`agent/`, `control-plane/`, `shared/`, `docs/`). Adding 5 more library directories there would make the root noisy and blur the distinction between application code and reusable libraries.
- A `libs/` directory creates a clear namespace: "these are standalone libraries that happen to live in the monorepo."
- The `shared/` package stays where it is since it is conceptually part of the application (StratoShared DTOs), not a standalone library.

**Why NOT merge them into a single package (e.g., a mega-`libs/Package.swift`):**

- Each library has different external dependencies, platform constraints, and swift-tools-versions. A single Package.swift would force the lowest common denominator or require complex conditional logic.
- Separate packages give independent build caches. A change to SwiftOVN does not invalidate the SwiftQEMU build cache.
- Libraries remain independently publishable to GitHub/SwiftPM registries if needed later.
- This exactly mirrors the existing `shared/` pattern, which is already proven in this repo.

---

### 2. Updating Package.swift Files (Consumers)

**`agent/Package.swift`** -- change two dependency declarations from remote Git URLs to local paths:

```swift
// Before:
.package(url: "https://github.com/samcat116/swift-qemu", branch: "main"),
.package(url: "https://github.com/samcat116/swift-ovn.git", branch: "main"),

// After:
.package(path: "../libs/swift-qemu"),
.package(path: "../libs/swift-ovn"),
```

All `.product(name: "SwiftQEMU", package: "swift-qemu")` and `.product(name: "SwiftOVN", package: "swift-ovn")` references in target dependencies remain unchanged because the package names in the library Package.swift files match.

**Note on `swift-ovn` package name:** The swift-ovn Package.swift declares `name: "SwiftOVN"` but the agent references it as `package: "swift-ovn"` (the URL-derived identity). When switching to a path dependency, SPM uses the `name` field from the package's Package.swift. You will need to verify whether the product reference `package: "swift-ovn"` still resolves correctly, or whether it needs to become `package: "SwiftOVN"`. The same applies to `swift-qemu` (package name is `"swift-qemu"`, product reference is `package: "swift-qemu"` -- this one matches).

Actually, looking more carefully: `swift-ovn`'s Package.swift declares `name: "SwiftOVN"`, but the agent references `.product(name: "SwiftOVN", package: "swift-ovn")`. With path dependencies, SPM identifies the package by its declared `name` field, not the directory name. So the agent reference would need to change to `package: "SwiftOVN"`.

For consistency, I recommend making the directory name match the package identity:
- `libs/swift-ovn/` with `name: "SwiftOVN"` -- agent references `package: "SwiftOVN"`
- `libs/swift-qemu/` with `name: "swift-qemu"` -- agent references `package: "swift-qemu"` (already matches)
- `libs/swift-scim/` with `name: "SwiftSCIM"` -- control-plane references `package: "swift-scim"` which needs to become `package: "SwiftSCIM"`

**`control-plane/Package.swift`** -- change one dependency declaration:

```swift
// Before:
.package(url: "https://github.com/samcat116/swift-scim.git", branch: "main"),

// After:
.package(path: "../libs/swift-scim"),
```

And update the product reference from `package: "swift-scim"` to `package: "SwiftSCIM"` (the declared name).

**Library Package.swift files** -- no changes needed to the libraries' own Package.swift files. They continue to declare their external dependencies (swift-nio, swift-log, etc.) via URL. SPM deduplicates these automatically when resolving the dependency graph.

---

### 3. Swift Tools Version Considerations

Current versions:
- `control-plane`: 6.1
- `agent`: 6.0
- `shared`: 6.0
- `swift-ovn`: 5.9
- `swift-qemu`: 6.0
- `swift-scim`: 6.1
- `swift-ssf`: 5.9

**This is not a problem.** SPM handles mixed swift-tools-versions across packages in a dependency graph gracefully. Each package is compiled according to its own declared tools version. A 6.1 consumer can depend on a 5.9 library with no issues.

**However**, I recommend bumping `swift-ovn` and `swift-ssf` from 5.9 to 6.0 as part of the import, because:
- The monorepo CI is already building with Swift 6.1+ (the Dockerfiles use `swift:6.1.2-noble`).
- Swift 6 strict concurrency is enabled across the project (`swiftLanguageModes: [.v6]`).
- Libraries at 5.9 may produce concurrency warnings when consumed by Swift 6 code. Better to address this upfront.
- The agent already uses `swiftLanguageModes: [.v5]`, so there is some flexibility, but aligning the libraries toward 6.0 is the right direction.

This is optional and can be done in a follow-up PR if the import itself is large enough.

---

### 4. Git History Preservation

**Recommended: `git subtree add`**

This is the standard approach for absorbing external repos into a monorepo while preserving commit history:

```bash
# For each library:
git remote add swift-ovn-remote /Users/sam/Projects/Active/swift-ovn
git fetch swift-ovn-remote
git subtree add --prefix=libs/swift-ovn swift-ovn-remote main --squash

git remote add swift-qemu-remote /Users/sam/Projects/Active/swift-qemu
git fetch swift-qemu-remote
git subtree add --prefix=libs/swift-qemu swift-qemu-remote main --squash

git remote add swift-scim-remote /Users/sam/Projects/Active/swift-scim
git fetch swift-scim-remote
git subtree add --prefix=libs/swift-scim swift-scim-remote main --squash

git remote add swift-ssf-remote /Users/sam/Projects/Active/swift-ssf
git fetch swift-ssf-remote
git subtree add --prefix=libs/swift-ssf swift-ssf-remote main --squash

# Clean up temporary remotes
git remote remove swift-ovn-remote
git remote remove swift-qemu-remote
git remote remove swift-scim-remote
git remote remove swift-ssf-remote
```

The `--squash` flag collapses each library's history into a single merge commit, keeping the monorepo log clean while still recording the origin. If you want full commit-by-commit history, omit `--squash`, but this will interleave all the library commits into `git log` output.

**Alternative considered: simple copy.** Just copy the files and make a fresh commit. Simpler, but loses all history. Given that these libraries have modest histories (5 commits for swift-ovn, ~20 for swift-qemu, 1 each for swift-scim and swift-ssf), either approach is fine. The subtree approach is cleaner for attribution.

**What to exclude from import:** Each library has `.build/`, `.DS_Store`, `.claude/`, `.entire/`, `.conductor/` directories that should not be imported. After the subtree add, clean these up in a follow-up commit, and ensure `.gitignore` covers them (the existing `.gitignore` already excludes `.build` and `.claude/`).

---

**swift-spiffe (empty, no git repo):**
- Do NOT import it. There is no code, no Package.swift, no git history. It is just a `.claude/` directory.
- Create a minimal placeholder only if you want to reserve the directory structure for future development:
  ```
  libs/swift-spiffe/
    Package.swift    (minimal, no targets)
    README.md        (notes that this is a future library)
  ```
- My recommendation: skip it entirely. Add it when there is actual code to import. Placeholder directories create confusion.

**swift-ssf (complete but unused in strato):**
- Import it into `libs/swift-ssf/` alongside the others. It has real code, tests, and a complete Package.swift.
- Do NOT add it as a dependency to any consuming package yet. Just bring it into the monorepo so it lives alongside the other libraries and benefits from unified CI.
- This matches the principle of "monorepo as the single source of truth" -- even if code is not yet wired up, it should live here if it belongs to the project.

---

### 6. Dockerfile Updates

Both Dockerfiles already handle the `shared` package with a `sed` command to rewrite the relative path. The same pattern extends to the new libs:

**`agent/Dockerfile`** -- add sed commands for the new library paths:

```dockerfile
# Copy libs into the build context
COPY ./libs/swift-qemu ./libs/swift-qemu
COPY ./libs/swift-ovn ./libs/swift-ovn

# Rewrite paths
RUN sed -i 's|.package(path: "../libs/swift-qemu")|.package(path: "/build/libs/swift-qemu")|' Package.swift
RUN sed -i 's|.package(path: "../libs/swift-ovn")|.package(path: "/build/libs/swift-ovn")|' Package.swift
```

**`control-plane/Dockerfile`** -- same pattern for swift-scim:

```dockerfile
COPY ./libs/swift-scim ./libs/swift-scim
RUN sed -i 's|.package(path: "../libs/swift-scim")|.package(path: "/build/libs/swift-scim")|' Package.swift
```

**Docker build context note:** Both Dockerfiles are built from the monorepo root (the `COPY ./shared ./shared` lines confirm this). The `libs/` directory will naturally be in the build context.

---

### 7. CI Workflow Updates

**`.github/workflows/build.yaml`** (PR Validation):

The cache key currently hashes `control-plane/Package.resolved` and `shared/Package.resolved`. After bringing in libs, update to include their resolved files too:

```yaml
key: ${{ runner.os }}-swift-${{ hashFiles('control-plane/Package.resolved', 'shared/Package.resolved', 'libs/*/Package.resolved') }}
```

Also add build steps for the libraries' own tests:

```yaml
- name: Build and Test SwiftOVN
  working-directory: libs/swift-ovn
  run: swift build --build-tests && swift test --skip-build
```

Note: SwiftOVN tests may need OVS/OVN infrastructure, so they might be Linux-only CI. SwiftQEMU tests need QEMU. Evaluate which library tests can run in CI and which are integration tests.

**`.github/workflows/main-build.yaml`** -- update the cache paths:

```yaml
path: |
  control-plane/.build
  agent/.build
  shared/.build
  libs/swift-ovn/.build
  libs/swift-qemu/.build
  libs/swift-scim/.build
  libs/swift-ssf/.build
```

---

### 8. Taskfile.yml Updates

Add convenience tasks for building/testing the libraries:

```yaml
build-libs:
  desc: Build all library packages
  cmds:
    - swift build --package-path "{{.PROJECT_ROOT}}/libs/swift-ovn"
    - swift build --package-path "{{.PROJECT_ROOT}}/libs/swift-qemu"
    - swift build --package-path "{{.PROJECT_ROOT}}/libs/swift-scim"
    - swift build --package-path "{{.PROJECT_ROOT}}/libs/swift-ssf"

test-libs:
  desc: Test all library packages
  cmds:
    - swift test --package-path "{{.PROJECT_ROOT}}/libs/swift-scim"
    - swift test --package-path "{{.PROJECT_ROOT}}/libs/swift-ssf"
    # swift-qemu and swift-ovn tests may need infrastructure
```

---

### 9. Cleanup Tasks After Import

After the subtree imports, in a follow-up commit:

1. **Delete imported artifacts that should not be in the monorepo:**
   - `libs/swift-ovn/.build/`, `libs/swift-ovn/.DS_Store`, `libs/swift-ovn/.claude/`, `libs/swift-ovn/.entire/`
   - Same for each library.
   - Delete `libs/swift-ovn/Examples/` directory and remove the `BasicUsage` executable target from its Package.swift (examples belong in docs, not in a monorepo dependency).
   - Delete `libs/swift-ssf/Sources/ExampleReceiver/` and remove the `ExampleReceiver` executable product and target from its Package.swift.
   - Delete any `.github/` directories from the libraries (they have their own CI that is superseded by the monorepo CI).

2. **Delete Package.resolved files** from each library. The monorepo's `.gitignore` already ignores `Package.resolved`. Each consuming package resolves its own dependency graph.

3. **Update AGENTS.md** to document the new `libs/` directory, build commands, and the relationship between libs and consumers.

4. **Archive the original external repos.** After verifying everything works in the monorepo, archive the GitHub repos for swift-ovn, swift-qemu, swift-scim, and swift-ssf to prevent drift. Optionally add a note to each repo's README pointing to the monorepo.

---

### 10. Step-by-Step Execution Sequence

**Phase 1: Import (one PR)**
1. Create the `libs/` directory.
2. Run `git subtree add` for swift-ovn, swift-qemu, swift-scim, swift-ssf.
3. Clean up imported artifacts (.build, .DS_Store, .claude, .entire, .conductor, .github, Examples, ExampleReceiver, Package.resolved).
4. Remove example executable targets from swift-ovn and swift-ssf Package.swift files.
5. Commit the cleanup.

**Phase 2: Wire up local paths (same PR or immediately following)**
6. Update `agent/Package.swift`: change swift-qemu and swift-ovn to path dependencies, fix `package:` identifiers.
7. Update `control-plane/Package.swift`: change swift-scim to path dependency, fix `package:` identifier.
8. Delete `agent/Package.resolved` and `control-plane/Package.resolved` (they will be regenerated on next resolve).
9. Run `swift build --package-path agent` and `swift build --package-path control-plane` to verify resolution.
10. Commit.

**Phase 3: Update infrastructure (same PR or follow-up)**
11. Update both Dockerfiles to copy libs and rewrite paths.
12. Update CI workflows (cache keys, build steps).
13. Update Taskfile.yml with lib build/test tasks.
14. Update AGENTS.md.
15. Commit and open PR.

**Phase 4: Validation**
16. Verify `swift build` works for agent, control-plane, shared, and each lib independently.
17. Verify `swift test` works for control-plane and each lib.
18. Verify Docker builds work.
19. Verify CI passes.

**Phase 5: Post-merge cleanup**
20. Archive external GitHub repos.

---

### Risks and Mitigations

| Risk | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|--------|------------|
| Package identity mismatch when switching from URL to path | Build fails | Verify `package:` references match the `name:` field in each library's Package.swift. Test locally before committing. |
| Dependency version conflicts (e.g., swift-nio version ranges) | Build fails | All libraries require swift-nio `from: "2.58.0"` or `from: "2.65.0"`. SPM resolves to the highest compatible version. No conflict expected. |
| swift-ovn Linux-only code fails to compile on macOS CI | CI failure | The agent already conditionally includes SwiftOVN only on Linux. The library itself compiles on macOS (it targets macOS(.v13)). Its tests may need OVN infrastructure though. |
| Large PR makes review difficult | Reviewer fatigue | The subtree import commits are mechanical. Phase 2 (path changes) is ~10 lines across 2 files. Keep cleanup separate from wiring. |

---

### Critical Files for Implementation
- `/Users/sam/Projects/Active/strato/agent/Package.swift` - Must change swift-qemu and swift-ovn from URL to local path dependencies, and fix package identity references
- `/Users/sam/Projects/Active/strato/control-plane/Package.swift` - Must change swift-scim from URL to local path dependency, and fix package identity reference
- `/Users/sam/Projects/Active/strato/agent/Dockerfile` - Must add COPY and sed commands for the new libs directory
- `/Users/sam/Projects/Active/strato/.github/workflows/build.yaml` - Must update cache keys and add lib build/test steps for CI
- `/Users/sam/Projects/Active/strato/control-plane/Dockerfile` - Must add COPY and sed commands for swift-scim via the libs directory

Now I have a thorough understanding of the entire codebase. Let me provide the complete implementation plan.

---

## Implementation Plan: Bringing 5 External Swift Libraries into the Strato Monorepo

### Executive Summary

The recommended approach is to place each library as a **separate SPM package** in a new `libs/` directory at the monorepo root, mirroring the existing pattern where `shared/` is its own package consumed via `.package(path: ...)`. Each library keeps its own `Package.swift`, `Sources/`, and `Tests/`, preserving independent buildability and testability. Git history is imported using `git subtree add`.

---

### 1. Directory Structure

Proposed layout:

```
strato/
  agent/                  (existing)
  control-plane/          (existing)
  shared/                 (existing)
  libs/
    swift-ovn/
      Package.swift
      Sources/SwiftOVN/
      Tests/SwiftOVNTests/
    swift-qemu/
      Package.swift
      Sources/SwiftQEMU/
      Tests/SwiftQEMUTests/
    swift-scim/
      Package.swift
      Sources/SwiftSCIM/
      Tests/SwiftSCIMTests/
    swift-ssf/
      Package.swift
      Sources/SwiftSSF/
      Tests/SwiftSSFTests/
    swift-spiffe/         (placeholder - minimal or empty)
```

**Why `libs/` rather than flat at the root:**

- The root already has 4 top-level directories that are conceptually "the app" (`agent/`, `control-plane/`, `shared/`, `docs/`). Adding 5 more library directories there would make the root noisy and blur the distinction between application code and reusable libraries.
- A `libs/` directory creates a clear namespace: "these are standalone libraries that happen to live in the monorepo."
- The `shared/` package stays where it is since it is conceptually part of the application (StratoShared DTOs), not a standalone library.

**Why NOT merge them into a single package (e.g., a mega-`libs/Package.swift`):**

- Each library has different external dependencies, platform constraints, and swift-tools-versions. A single Package.swift would force the lowest common denominator or require complex conditional logic.
- Separate packages give independent build caches. A change to SwiftOVN does not invalidate the SwiftQEMU build cache.
- Libraries remain independently publishable to GitHub/SwiftPM registries if needed later.
- This exactly mirrors the existing `shared/` pattern, which is already proven in this repo.

---

### 2. Updating Package.swift Files (Consumers)

**`agent/Package.swift`** -- change two dependency declarations from remote Git URLs to local paths:

```swift
// Before:
.package(url: "https://github.com/samcat116/swift-qemu", branch: "main"),
.package(url: "https://github.com/samcat116/swift-ovn.git", branch: "main"),

// After:
.package(path: "../libs/swift-qemu"),
.package(path: "../libs/swift-ovn"),
```

All `.product(name: "SwiftQEMU", package: "swift-qemu")` and `.product(name: "SwiftOVN", package: "swift-ovn")` references in target dependencies remain unchanged because the package names in the library Package.swift files match.

**Note on `swift-ovn` package name:** The swift-ovn Package.swift declares `name: "SwiftOVN"` but the agent references it as `package: "swift-ovn"` (the URL-derived identity). When switching to a path dependency, SPM uses the `name` field from the package's Package.swift. You will need to verify whether the product reference `package: "swift-ovn"` still resolves correctly, or whether it needs to become `package: "SwiftOVN"`. The same applies to `swift-qemu` (package name is `"swift-qemu"`, product reference is `package: "swift-qemu"` -- this one matches).

Actually, looking more carefully: `swift-ovn`'s Package.swift declares `name: "SwiftOVN"`, but the agent references `.product(name: "SwiftOVN", package: "swift-ovn")`. With path dependencies, SPM identifies the package by its declared `name` field, not the directory name. So the agent reference would need to change to `package: "SwiftOVN"`.

For consistency, I recommend making the directory name match the package identity:
- `libs/swift-ovn/` with `name: "SwiftOVN"` -- agent references `package: "SwiftOVN"`
- `libs/swift-qemu/` with `name: "swift-qemu"` -- agent references `package: "swift-qemu"` (already matches)
- `libs/swift-scim/` with `name: "SwiftSCIM"` -- control-plane references `package: "swift-scim"` which needs to become `package: "SwiftSCIM"`

**`control-plane/Package.swift`** -- change one dependency declaration:

```swift
// Before:
.package(url: "https://github.com/samcat116/swift-scim.git", branch: "main"),

// After:
.package(path: "../libs/swift-scim"),
```

And update the product reference from `package: "swift-scim"` to `package: "SwiftSCIM"` (the declared name).

**Library Package.swift files** -- no changes needed to the libraries' own Package.swift files. They continue to declare their external dependencies (swift-nio, swift-log, etc.) via URL. SPM deduplicates these automatically when resolving the dependency graph.

---

- `control-plane`: 6.1
- `agent`: 6.0
- `shared`: 6.0
- `swift-ovn`: 5.9
- `swift-qemu`: 6.0
- `swift-scim`: 6.1
- `swift-ssf`: 5.9

**This is not a problem.** SPM handles mixed swift-tools-versions across packages in a dependency graph gracefully. Each package is compiled according to its own declared tools version. A 6.1 consumer can depend on a 5.9 library with no issues.

**However**, I recommend bumping `swift-ovn` and `swift-ssf` from 5.9 to 6.0 as part of the import, because:
- The monorepo CI is already building with Swift 6.1+ (the Dockerfiles use `swift:6.1.2-noble`).
- Swift 6 strict concurrency is enabled across the project (`swiftLanguageModes: [.v6]`).

- The agent already uses `swiftLanguageModes: [.v5]`, so there is some flexibility, but aligning the libraries toward 6.0 is the right direction.

   - Delete `libs/swift-ovn/Examples/` directory and remove the `BasicUsage` executable target from its Package.swift (examples belong in docs, not in a monorepo dependency).
   - Delete `libs/swift-ssf/Sources/ExampleReceiver/` and remove the `ExampleReceiver` executable product and target from its Package.swift.
   - Delete any `.github/` directories from the libraries (they have their own CI that is superseded by the monorepo CI).

2. **Delete Package.resolved files** from each library. The monorepo's `.gitignore` already ignores `Package.resolved`. Each consuming package resolves its own dependency graph.

3. **Update AGENTS.md** to document the new `libs/` directory, build commands, and the relationship between libs and consumers.

4. **Archive the original external repos.** After verifying everything works in the monorepo, archive the GitHub repos for swift-ovn, swift-qemu, swift-scim, and swift-ssf to prevent drift. Optionally add a note to each repo's README pointing to the monorepo.

---

### 10. Step-by-Step Execution Sequence

**Phase 1: Import (one PR)**
1. Create the `libs/` directory.
2. Run `git subtree add` for swift-ovn, swift-qemu, swift-scim, swift-ssf.
3. Clean up imported artifacts (.build, .DS_Store, .claude, .entire, .conductor, .github, Examples, ExampleReceiver, Package.resolved).
4. Remove example executable targets from swift-ovn and swift-ssf Package.swift files.
5. Commit the cleanup.

**Phase 2: Wire up local paths (same PR or immediately following)**
6. Update `agent/Package.swift`: change swift-qemu and swift-ovn to path dependencies, fix `package:` identifiers.
7. Update `control-plane/Package.swift`: change swift-scim to path dependency, fix `package:` identifier.
8. Delete `agent/Package.resolved` and `control-plane/Package.resolved` (they will be regenerated on next resolve).
9. Run `swift build --package-path agent` and `swift build --package-path control-plane` to verify resolution.
10. Commit.

**Phase 3: Update infrastructure (same PR or follow-up)**
11. Update both Dockerfiles to copy libs and rewrite paths.
12. Update CI workflows (cache keys, build steps).
13. Update Taskfile.yml with lib build/test tasks.
14. Update AGENTS.md.
15. Commit and open PR.

**Phase 4: Validation**
16. Verify `swift build` works for agent, control-plane, shared, and each lib independently.
17. Verify `swift test` works for control-plane and each lib.
18. Verify Docker builds work.
19. Verify CI passes.

**Phase 5: Post-merge cleanup**
20. Archive external GitHub repos.

---

### Risks and Mitigations

| Risk | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|--------|------------|
| Package identity mismatch when switching from URL to path | Build fails | Verify `package:` references match the `name:` field in each library's Package.swift. Test locally before committing. |
| Dependency version conflicts (e.g., swift-nio version ranges) | Build fails | All libraries require swift-nio `from: "2.58.0"` or `from: "2.65.0"`. SPM resolves to the highest compatible version. No conflict expected. |
| swift-ovn Linux-only code fails to compile on macOS CI | CI failure | The agent already conditionally includes SwiftOVN only on Linux. The library itself compiles on macOS (it targets macOS(.v13)). Its tests may need OVN infrastructure though. |
| Large PR makes review difficult | Reviewer fatigue | The subtree import commits are mechanical. Phase 2 (path changes) is ~10 lines across 2 files. Keep cleanup separate from wiring. |

---

### Critical Files for Implementation
- `/Users/sam/Projects/Active/strato/agent/Package.swift` - Must change swift-qemu and swift-ovn from URL to local path dependencies, and fix package identity references
- `/Users/sam/Projects/Active/strato/control-plane/Package.swift` - Must change swift-scim from URL to local path dependency, and fix package identity reference
- `/Users/sam/Projects/Active/strato/agent/Dockerfile` - Must add COPY and sed commands for the new libs directory
- `/Users/sam/Projects/Active/strato/.github/workflows/build.yaml` - Must update cache keys and add lib build/test steps for CI
- `/Users/sam/Projects/Active/strato/control-plane/Dockerfile` - Must add COPY and sed commands for swift-scim via the libs directory
Let me verify the package identity details before finalizing the plan.

// swift-tools-version: 5.9
// The swift-tools-version declares the minimum version of Swift required to build this package.

import PackageDescription

let package = Package(
    name: "SwiftOVN",
    platforms: [
        .macOS(.v13),
        .iOS(.v16),
        .watchOS(.v9),
        .tvOS(.v16),
        .visionOS(.v1)
    ],
    products: [
        .library(
            name: "SwiftOVN",
            targets: ["SwiftOVN"]),
    ],
    dependencies: [
        .package(url: "https://github.com/apple/swift-nio.git", from: "2.65.0"),
        .package(url: "https://github.com/apple/swift-log.git", from: "1.0.0"),
    ],
    targets: [
        .target(
            name: "SwiftOVN",
            dependencies: [
                .product(name: "NIO", package: "swift-nio"),
                .product(name: "NIOPosix", package: "swift-nio"),
                .product(name: "NIOFoundationCompat", package: "swift-nio"),
                .product(name: "Logging", package: "swift-log"),
            ]
        ),
        .executableTarget(
            name: "BasicUsage",
            dependencies: ["SwiftOVN"],
            path: "Examples"
        ),
        .testTarget(
            name: "SwiftOVNTests",
            dependencies: ["SwiftOVN"]
        ),
    ]
)

// swift-tools-version: 5.9
// The swift-tools-version declares the minimum version of Swift required to build this package.

import PackageDescription

let package = Package(
    name: "SwiftSSF",
    platforms: [
        .macOS(.v10_15),
        .iOS(.v13),
        .watchOS(.v6),
        .tvOS(.v13),
        .visionOS(.v1)
    ],
    products: [
        .library(
            name: "SwiftSSF",
            targets: ["SwiftSSF"]
        ),
        .executable(
            name: "ExampleReceiver",
            targets: ["ExampleReceiver"]
        ),
    ],
    dependencies: [
        .package(url: "https://github.com/apple/swift-nio.git", from: "2.58.0"),
        .package(url: "https://github.com/apple/swift-crypto.git", from: "3.0.0"),
        .package(url: "https://github.com/apple/swift-log.git", from: "1.5.0"),
        .package(url: "https://github.com/swift-server/async-http-client.git", from: "1.19.0"),
        .package(url: "https://github.com/apple/swift-nio-http2.git", from: "1.28.0")
    ],
    targets: [
        .target(
            name: "SwiftSSF",
            dependencies: [
                .product(name: "NIO", package: "swift-nio"),
                .product(name: "NIOHTTP1", package: "swift-nio"),
                .product(name: "NIOFoundationCompat", package: "swift-nio"),
                .product(name: "NIOConcurrencyHelpers", package: "swift-nio"),
                .product(name: "Crypto", package: "swift-crypto"),
                .product(name: "Logging", package: "swift-log"),
                .product(name: "AsyncHTTPClient", package: "async-http-client"),
                .product(name: "NIOHTTP2", package: "swift-nio-http2")
            ]
        ),
        .executableTarget(
            name: "ExampleReceiver",
            dependencies: ["SwiftSSF"]
        ),
        .testTarget(
            name: "SwiftSSFTests",
            dependencies: ["SwiftSSF"]
        ),
    ]
)

file.content:
# ================================
# Build image
# ================================
FROM swift:6.1.2-noble AS build

# Install OS updates
RUN export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive DEBCONF_NONINTERACTIVE_SEEN=true \
    && apt-get -q update \
    && apt-get -q dist-upgrade -y \
    && apt-get install -y libjemalloc-dev

# Set up a build area
WORKDIR /build

# Copy shared package first
COPY ./shared ./shared

# Copy agent package files
COPY ./agent/Package.* ./agent/

# First just resolve dependencies.
# This creates a cached layer that can be reused
# as long as your Package.swift/Package.resolved
# files do not change.
WORKDIR /build/agent
# Update Package.swift to use absolute path for shared package in Docker
RUN sed -i 's|.package(path: "../shared")|.package(path: "/build/shared")|' Package.swift
RUN swift package resolve \
    $([ -f ./Package.resolved ] && echo "--force-resolved-versions" || true)

# Copy entire agent repo into container
COPY ./agent .

# Build the application statically, with optimizations and using jemalloc
RUN swift build -c release \
    --product StratoAgent \
    -Xlinker -ljemalloc

# Switch to the staging area
WORKDIR /staging

# Copy main executable to staging area
RUN cp "$(swift build --package-path /build/agent -c release --show-bin-path)/StratoAgent" ./

# Copy static swift backtracer binary to staging area (try both possible locations)
RUN cp "/usr/lib/swift/linux/swift-backtrace-static" ./ 2>/dev/null || \
    cp "/usr/libexec/swift/linux/swift-backtrace-static" ./ 2>/dev/null || \
    echo "Warning: swift-backtrace-static not found"

# Copy resources bundled by SPM to staging area
RUN find -L "$(swift build --package-path /build/agent -c release --show-bin-path)/" -regex '.*\.resources$' -exec cp -Ra {} ./ \;

# ================================
# Run image
# ================================
FROM ubuntu:noble

# Make sure all system packages are up to date, and install QEMU and essential packages.
RUN export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive DEBCONF_NONINTERACTIVE_SEEN=true \
    && apt-get -q update \
    && apt-get -q dist-upgrade -y \
    && apt-get -q install -y \
    libjemalloc2 \
    ca-certificates \
    tzdata \
    socat \
    # QEMU and KVM for virtualization
    qemu-system-x86 \
    qemu-utils \
    # glib-2.0 development files for QEMUKit
    libglib2.0-0 \
    libglib2.0-dev \
    # OVN/OVS for software-defined networking
    ovn-central \
    ovn-host \
    ovn-common \
    openvswitch-switch \
    openvswitch-common \
    # If your app or its dependencies import FoundationNetworking, also install `libcurl4`.
    # libcurl4 \
    # If your app or its dependencies import FoundationXML, also install `libxml2`.
    # libxml2 \
    && rm -r /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# No need to copy Swift runtime libraries with static compilation

# Create a strato user and group with /app as its home directory
RUN useradd --user-group --create-home --system --skel /dev/null --home-dir /app strato

# Switch to the new home directory
WORKDIR /app

# Copy built executable and any staged resources from builder
COPY --from=build --chown=strato:strato /staging /app

# Provide configuration needed by the built-in crash reporter and some sensible default behaviors.
ENV SWIFT_BACKTRACE=enable=yes,sanitize=yes,threads=all,images=all,interactive=no,swift-backtrace=./swift-backtrace-static

# Ensure all further commands run as the strato user
USER strato:strato

# Start the Strato Agent when the image is run
ENTRYPOINT ["./StratoAgent"]
CMD ["--control-plane-url", "ws://control-plane:8080/agent/ws", "--qemu-socket-dir", "/var/run/qemu"]

file.content:
# ================================
# Build image - only for local builds
# ================================
FROM swift:6.1.2-noble AS build

# Check if we're using prebuilt artifacts
ARG PREBUILT_ARTIFACTS=""

# Install OS updates and Bun for frontend build
RUN export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive DEBCONF_NONINTERACTIVE_SEEN=true \
    && apt-get -q update \
    && apt-get -q dist-upgrade -y \
    && apt-get install -y libjemalloc-dev curl unzip \
    && curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash

# Add Bun to PATH
ENV BUN_INSTALL="/root/.bun"
ENV PATH="$BUN_INSTALL/bin:$PATH"

# Set up a build area
WORKDIR /build

# Copy shared package first
COPY ./shared ./shared

# Copy control plane package files
COPY ./control-plane/Package.* ./control-plane/

# First just resolve dependencies.
WORKDIR /build/control-plane
# Update Package.swift to use absolute path for shared package in Docker
RUN sed -i 's|.package(path: "../shared")|.package(path: "/build/shared")|' Package.swift
RUN swift package resolve \
    $([ -f ./Package.resolved ] && echo "--force-resolved-versions" || true)

# Copy web frontend package files
COPY ./control-plane/web/package.json ./control-plane/web/bun.lock* ./web/

# Install frontend dependencies
WORKDIR /build/control-plane/web
RUN bun install --frozen-lockfile

# Copy entire control-plane repo into container
WORKDIR /build/control-plane
COPY ./control-plane .

# Copy artifacts directory (always exists due to workflow preparation)
COPY ./artifacts /build/artifacts

# Build and deploy frontend (Next.js static export to Public/)
WORKDIR /build/control-plane/web
RUN bun run build:deploy

WORKDIR /build/control-plane

# Build the application only if not using prebuilt artifacts
RUN if [ -z "$PREBUILT_ARTIFACTS" ]; then \
        swift build -c release --product App -Xlinker -ljemalloc; \
    fi

# Switch to the staging area
WORKDIR /staging

# If using prebuilt artifacts, copy them, otherwise copy from build
RUN if [ -n "$PREBUILT_ARTIFACTS" ]; then \
        echo "Using prebuilt artifacts mode"; \
        if [ -d "/build/artifacts/control-plane" ]; then \
            echo "Found prebuilt artifacts"; \
            # Copy binaries from bin/ subdirectory
            if [ -d "/build/artifacts/control-plane/bin" ]; then \
                cp /build/artifacts/control-plane/bin/* ./; \
            else \
                cp /build/artifacts/control-plane/App ./ 2>/dev/null || (echo "Error: App executable not found in artifacts"; exit 1); \
            fi; \
            # Copy resources if they exist
            ([ -d "/build/artifacts/control-plane/resources" ] && cp -R /build/artifacts/control-plane/resources/* ./ || true) && \
            ([ -d "/build/artifacts/control-plane/Public" ] && cp -R /build/artifacts/control-plane/Public ./ || true) && \
            ([ -d "/build/artifacts/control-plane/Resources" ] && cp -R /build/artifacts/control-plane/Resources ./ || true); \
        else \
            echo "Error: PREBUILT_ARTIFACTS specified but no artifacts found"; \
            exit 1; \
        fi; \
    else \
        echo "Building from source"; \
        cp "$(swift build --package-path /build/control-plane -c release --show-bin-path)/App" ./ && \
        (cp "/usr/lib/swift/linux/swift-backtrace-static" ./ 2>/dev/null || cp "/usr/libexec/swift/linux/swift-backtrace-static" ./ 2>/dev/null || echo "Warning: swift-backtrace-static not found") && \
        find -L "$(swift build --package-path /build/control-plane -c release --show-bin-path)/" -regex '.*\.resources$' -exec cp -Ra {} ./ \; && \
        ([ -d /build/control-plane/Public ] && cp -R /build/control-plane/Public ./ || true) && \
        ([ -d /build/control-plane/Resources ] && cp -R /build/control-plane/Resources ./ || true); \
    fi

# Ensure proper permissions
RUN chmod +x ./App && \
    chmod -R a-w ./Public 2>/dev/null || true && \
    chmod -R a-w ./Resources 2>/dev/null || true

# ================================
# Run image
# ================================
FROM ubuntu:noble

# Make sure all system packages are up to date, and install only essential packages.
RUN export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive DEBCONF_NONINTERACTIVE_SEEN=true \
    && apt-get -q update \
    && apt-get -q dist-upgrade -y \
    && apt-get -q install -y \
    libjemalloc2 \
    ca-certificates \
    tzdata \
    # If your app or its dependencies import FoundationNetworking, also install `libcurl4`.
    # libcurl4 \
    # If your app or its dependencies import FoundationXML, also install `libxml2`.
    # libxml2 \
    && rm -r /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy Swift runtime libraries from build stage
COPY --from=build /usr/lib/swift/linux/*.so /usr/lib/

# Create a vapor user and group with /app as its home directory
RUN useradd --user-group --create-home --system --skel /dev/null --home-dir /app vapor

# Switch to the new home directory
WORKDIR /app

# Copy built executable and any staged resources from builder
COPY --from=build --chown=vapor:vapor /staging /app

# Provide configuration needed by the built-in crash reporter and some sensible default behaviors.
ENV SWIFT_BACKTRACE=enable=yes,sanitize=yes,threads=all,images=all,interactive=no,swift-backtrace=./swift-backtrace-static

# Ensure all further commands run as the vapor user
USER vapor:vapor

# Let Docker bind to port 8080
EXPOSE 8080

# Start the Vapor service when the image is run, default to listening on 8080 in production environment
ENTRYPOINT ["./App"]
CMD ["serve", "--env", "production", "--hostname", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8080"]

input.content:
# Plan: Bring External Swift Libraries into Strato Monorepo

## Context

Five external Swift libraries (`swift-ovn`, `swift-qemu`, `swift-scim`, `swift-spiffe`, `swift-ssf`) currently live in separate repos at `/Users/sam/Projects/Active/`. Three of them are already consumed by strato as git URL dependencies (swift-ovn, swift-qemu, swift-scim). The goal is to consolidate them into the monorepo for easier management, following the same local-path pattern already used by `shared/`.

## Approach: `libs/` directory with local path dependencies

Place each library as a separate SPM package under a new `libs/` directory. Each keeps its own `Package.swift`, `Sources/`, and `Tests/`. This mirrors the existing `shared/` pattern.

**Why `libs/` and not root-level?** The root already has `agent/`, `control-plane/`, `shared/`, `docs/`, etc. Adding 4-5 more library dirs would blur the line between app code and reusable libraries.

**Why separate packages, not merged?** Different swift-tools-versions, platform constraints, and external deps. Separate packages give independent build caches and keep libraries independently publishable if needed.

### Target structure

```
strato/
  libs/
    swift-ovn/        # Used by agent (Linux only)
    swift-qemu/       # Used by agent
    swift-scim/       # Used by control-plane
    swift-ssf/        # Not yet wired up, just colocated
```

> **swift-spiffe** is excluded — it has no code, no Package.swift, nothing to import.

## Steps

### 1. Import libraries via `git subtree add`

Preserves commit history while keeping the monorepo log clean with `--squash`:

```bash
git remote add swift-ovn-remote /Users/sam/Projects/Active/swift-ovn
git fetch swift-ovn-remote
git subtree add --prefix=libs/swift-ovn swift-ovn-remote main --squash

git remote add swift-qemu-remote /Users/sam/Projects/Active/swift-qemu
git fetch swift-qemu-remote
git subtree add --prefix=libs/swift-qemu swift-qemu-remote main --squash

git remote add swift-scim-remote /Users/sam/Projects/Active/swift-scim
git fetch swift-scim-remote
git subtree add --prefix=libs/swift-scim swift-scim-remote main --squash

git remote add swift-ssf-remote /Users/sam/Projects/Active/swift-ssf
git fetch swift-ssf-remote
git subtree add --prefix=libs/swift-ssf swift-ssf-remote main --squash

# Cleanup remotes
git remote remove swift-ovn-remote
git remote remove swift-qemu-remote
git remote remove swift-scim-remote
git remote remove swift-ssf-remote
```

### 2. Clean up imported artifacts

Remove from each `libs/<lib>/`: `.build/`, `.DS_Store`, `.claude/`, `.entire/`, `.conductor/`, `.github/`, `Package.resolved`.

Remove example executable targets:
- `libs/swift-ovn/Examples/` dir + `BasicUsage` target from its Package.swift
- `libs/swift-ssf/Sources/ExampleReceiver/` dir + `ExampleReceiver` product/target from its Package.swift

### 3. Update consumer Package.swift files

**`agent/Package.swift`** — change URL deps to local paths:
```swift
// Before
.package(url: "https://github.com/samcat116/swift-qemu", branch: "main"),
.package(url: "https://github.com/samcat116/swift-ovn.git", branch: "main"),

// After
.package(path: "../libs/swift-qemu"),
.package(path: "../libs/swift-ovn"),
```

**Package identity fix:** `swift-ovn` declares `name: "SwiftOVN"` in its Package.swift, but the agent references `package: "swift-ovn"` (URL-derived identity). With path deps, SPM uses the declared `name`. Fix: update the product reference in the `#if os(Linux)` block:
```swift
// Before
.product(name: "SwiftOVN", package: "swift-ovn"),
// After
.product(name: "SwiftOVN", package: "SwiftOVN"),
```
